title: 5 takeaways from Patriots’ first 2026 OTA practice
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye and offense had a solid start; Maye completed 13-of-22 passes with one interception.
Christian Gonzalez and Kayshon Boutte absent from practice; Gonzalez's contract extension talks continue.
Offensive line features new adjustments; Caleb Lomu practiced at right tackle, replacing Morgan Moses.

title: Woody’s Grill & Tap in Fenway will permanently close
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Woody's Grill & Tap in Fenway, known for wood-fired pizza, will close permanently Thursday night.
Located at 58 Hemenway St., Woody's has been a community venue near Fenway Park for years.
The closure announcement on Reddit spurred nostalgia, with many sharing fond memories of the restaurant.

title: A royal family dined at a popular Somerville restaurant this week
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Belgium's royal family dined at Sarma Restaurant in Somerville during a visit for Princess Elisabeth's Harvard graduation.
Princess Elisabeth, heir to the Belgian throne, earned a master's degree in public policy from Harvard Kennedy School.
Sarma, a popular mezze restaurant, is known for its long waitlist and Mediterranean-inspired dishes.

title: What to know about the rejection of Healey’s Parole Board nominee as backlash builds
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor's Council rejected Vincent DeMore's nomination to Massachusetts Parole Board after a close vote and public debate.
Governor Maura Healey and state district attorneys backed DeMore, citing his diverse legal experience and public safety focus.
Rejection follows increased scrutiny of Parole Board decisions after a Cambridge shooting by a parolee earlier this month.

title: Mike Vrabel talks offseason distractions, potential trades, and more
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Mike Vrabel addressed media questions on his offseason controversies and emphasized his focus on family and coaching.
Reports suggest the Patriots may soon trade for A.J. Brown, though Vrabel provided no confirmation during the press event.
Vrabel discussed the challenging 2026 Patriots schedule featuring six nationally televised games, including a match in Munich.

title: Video shows Sullivan’s Tap patrons running for cover when shots fired near TD Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Gunshots were fired near TD Garden around 10:45 p.m. Tuesday, causing patrons of Sullivan's Tap to seek cover.
No one was injured in the incident, but Hotel Indigo's front door and a parked vehicle were damaged by gunshots.
Police recovered ballistic evidence and are investigating the shooting; no arrests have been made yet.

title: Wu cancels Harvard speaking engagement rather than cross grad-student picket line
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu canceled her Harvard Law speaking event to avoid crossing a graduate student picket line.
Harvard graduate students have been striking since April 21 due to prolonged contract negotiations.
Wu, an advocate for labor movements, proposed alternatives but ultimately respected the picket line by withdrawing.

title: Mike’s Pastry is coming to Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Mike's Pastry to open in Terminal C at Logan Airport later this year, announced by MarketPlace Development.
The bakery will offer filled-to-order cannoli, cheesecake slices, lobster tails, and a full espresso bar.
Mike's Pastry at Logan will operate daily from 5 a.m. to 10 p.m. in a new blue, white, and gold space.

title: ‘Stranger Things’ actor Joe Keery joins star-studded cast for Broadway debut
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Joe Keery and Julia Louis-Dreyfus will debut on Broadway in 'Other Desert Cities' starting September 29, 2026.
The cast also includes Allison Janney, Ed Harris, and Lily Rabe in a 16-week run of the production.
The play explores a family's hidden scandals surfacing due to a daughter planning to publish a revealing memoir.

title: Police close South Boston beach after ‘crowds of youth’ break into fights
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Carson Beach and nearby Castle Island were closed after multiple fights involving youth on Tuesday evening.
Massachusetts State Police responded to reports of fights, loud music, and alcohol use at Carson Beach around 5:30 p.m.
No injuries or arrests occurred, and the area was cleared by 9:30 p.m. with parking lots and Day Boulevard later reopened.
